message:
perf(pager): short-circuit freelist dirty check on clean append growth

\`SimpleTransaction::commit\` (and the retain-fast-path) currently
compute \`freelist_metadata_dirty_with_inner\` unconditionally
before making the no-op-commit decision. That predicate walks the
full durable freelist projection — cheap on small DBs, noticeable
cost on large ones — and is wasted work in the very common case
of a transaction that grew the DB purely by appending pages (no
freed pages, no freelist allocations, no returned lease pages):
such a transaction CANNOT have changed freelist metadata, by
construction.

Add a cheap pre-gate and invoke it from both commit paths.

\`freelist_metadata_dirty_after_lease_return_with_inner(inner,
committed_db_size, returned_lease_pages)\`:

- Short-circuits to \`false\` when \`!returned_lease_pages &&
  freed_pages.is_empty() && allocated_from_freelist.is_empty()\`.
  This captures the append-only case exactly: no lease return,
  no freed page enqueue, no freelist consumption → freelist
  metadata CANNOT have moved.
- Otherwise delegates to the existing
  \`freelist_metadata_dirty_with_inner\`, preserving the full
  durable-projection comparison in every path that previously
  relied on it. No change to the logic for cases where the
  short-circuit wouldn't fire.

Two call sites updated:

- WAL split-lock commit path. Captures
  \`returned_lease_pages\` from \`!self.page_lease.is_empty()\`
  BEFORE the \`return_pages_to_freelist\` drain (the drain
  empties the lease, so the flag must be snapshotted first) and
  passes it into the new predicate.

- Retain-fast-path commit. Same snapshot + forward, so the
  fast-path's first decision (metadata-only single-connection
  fast path gated on \`!freelist_dirty_for_retain\`) gets the
  correct answer without the full projection walk on append-
  only workloads.

Regression tests added:

- \`test_freelist_dirty_after_lease_return_short_circuits_clean_append_growth\`
  Opens a WAL pager, begins a Concurrent transaction, allocates
  and writes ONE page (append-only growth), and directly calls
  the new predicate with \`returned_lease_pages = false\`.
  Asserts it returns \`false\` WITHOUT walking the full
  freelist projection. Includes the bead id in the assert
  message (\`bead_id=...\`) so grep of test failures points
  back to this work.

- \`test_freelist_dirty_after_lease_return_keeps_durable_lease_holes\`
  Pins the negative half: when the batched allocator leaves
  real lease holes in the freelist, the predicate must still
  return \`true\` after the lease return. Allocates three
  pages from the batched allocator (which leaves holes), then
  drains the lease back into the inner freelist and calls the
  predicate with \`returned_lease_pages = true\`. Asserts
  dirty. Guarantees the short-circuit never hides a real
  metadata change.

Pure performance lever. No semantic change: when the short-
circuit returns \`false\`, the existing predicate would also have
returned \`false\`; when it delegates, the existing predicate is
the one that answers.
